enrichment.short_description: JivoChat is an omnichannel business messenger unifying website chat, WhatsApp, Telegram, Instagram, and email in one shared inbox.
enrichment.seo_title: JivoChat Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: JivoChat review: omnichannel live chat, WhatsApp, Telegram and CRM in one inbox. See 2026 pricing tiers, real features, pros, cons and the best alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: JivoChat is an omnichannel business messenger and live chat platform that lets companies talk to website visitors and customers across chat widgets, WhatsApp, Telegram, Instagram, Facebook Messenger, Viber, email and phone calls from a single shared team inbox. The product was created in 2012 by Timur Valishev, who launched the service (originally branded JivoSite) out of Russia to give small and mid-sized online businesses an easy way to add proactive live chat to their websites; it grew into one of the most widely installed live chat widgets globally, with vendor-reported figures citing more than 180,000 active business customers. In June 2021 Russian banking group Sberbank acquired JivoChat for a reported 20.5 million dollars, folding it into Sberbank's broader e-commerce and SME software portfolio. Today the company operates internationally with a significant share of its team based in Vitoria, Brazil, reflecting JivoChat's large footprint in Latin American markets alongside its original CIS user base; some corporate registration records also list a Wilmington, Delaware entity, a common structure for internationally operating SaaS companies. Technically, JivoChat ships as an embeddable JavaScript widget for websites plus native desktop apps (Windows, macOS), mobile apps (iOS, Android), and a browser-based agent console, all synced in real time through JivoChat's own backend so a single agent can answer a chat, WhatsApp message, and email from one screen. Core features include a unified multichannel inbox, canned responses and an automatic assistant with ready-made answers, proactive chat invitations and visitor monitoring that shows what page a visitor is on in real time, a callback widget that lets visitors request a phone call, offline contact forms, a lightweight built-in CRM for tracking leads and conversation history, chatbot building tools, an AI Agent add-on that can auto-answer common questions, chat routing and departments for larger teams, a Telephony Plus module for click-to-call, video calls, and a Chat API and mobile SDK for embedding chat inside native apps. Pricing is freemium: the Free plan supports up to 5 agents with core website chat, callback, offline forms and the major social integrations at no cost. Paid tiers are billed per agent per month (cheaper on annual billing): Basic at roughly 28 dollars/agent/month adds proactive invitations, visitor monitoring, the automatic assistant and CRM for up to 2 agents; Professional at roughly 42 dollars/agent/month unlocks WhatsApp integration, voice messages, chatbots and chat rescue for up to 100 agents; and Enterprise at roughly 56 dollars/agent/month adds the mobile SDK, chat routing, video calls and the Chat API with unlimited agents. Optional add-on modules (WhatsApp connector, AI Assistant, Telephony Plus, video calls, Jivo Marketing) are priced separately, and a 14-day free trial is offered on paid plans. JivoChat targets small and medium-sized e-commerce stores, service businesses, and support teams that want an affordable, quick-to-install alternative to enterprise help-desk suites. It competes directly with Tidio, Tawk.to, LiveChat, Intercom and Zendesk Chat; JivoChat differentiates on its generous free tier, native multichannel messaging (especially WhatsApp and Telegram) bundled into the product, and pricing aimed at cost-conscious SMBs rather than large enterprise support organizations. Reported G2 ratings sit around 4.6 out of 5, with reviewers praising ease of setup and the multichannel inbox, while common complaints cite WhatsApp requiring a paid Twilio-based connector rather than a native integration, occasional notification overload, and slower support response times on lower tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: JivoChat began in 2012 as JivoSite, a website live chat widget built to help small online businesses talk to visitors in real time. Founder Timur Valishev grew it into a broader omnichannel messenger that now pulls together website chat, WhatsApp, Telegram, Instagram, Facebook Messenger, Viber, email and phone calls into one inbox, so a support or sales team never has to jump between separate apps to answer a customer.

The company was acquired by Russian banking group Sberbank in 2021 and now operates with a large team based in Vitoria, Brazil, reflecting its strong adoption among Latin American online retailers alongside its original user base. JivoChat reports over 180,000 active business customers and ships desktop, mobile and browser apps that stay synced in real time.

JivoChat is aimed squarely at small and mid-sized businesses that want proactive, affordable live chat rather than a full enterprise help-desk suite, competing with tools like Tidio, Tawk.to, LiveChat and Intercom on price and ease of setup.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: At its core, JivoChat is a website chat widget paired with a shared team inbox: agents see real-time visitor activity, can trigger proactive invitations, and can hand off conversations between departments. A built-in lightweight CRM keeps lead and conversation history attached to each contact, and canned responses plus an automatic assistant with ready-made answers speed up repetitive replies.

Beyond the chat widget, JivoChat layers on multichannel messaging (WhatsApp, Telegram, Instagram, Facebook, Viber), a callback widget, offline contact forms, chatbot building tools, and an optional AI Agent add-on that can auto-answer common customer questions. Higher tiers add a Telephony Plus click-to-call module, video calls, chat routing across departments, a Chat API, and a mobile SDK for embedding chat directly into native iOS/Android apps.

Because everything ships as one connected platform with native desktop, mobile and browser apps, teams can staff a single queue that spans channels rather than juggling separate WhatsApp Business, Instagram DM, and email tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: JivoChat uses a freemium, per-agent-per-month pricing model. The Free plan covers up to 5 agents with website chat, callback, offline forms and basic social integrations. Paid plans are billed per agent (discounted on annual billing): Basic (~$28/agent/month) adds proactive invitations, visitor monitoring and CRM for up to 2 agents; Professional (~$42/agent/month) unlocks WhatsApp, chatbots and voice messages for up to 100 agents; and Enterprise (~$56/agent/month) adds the mobile SDK, chat routing, video calls and Chat API with unlimited agents.

Several capabilities are sold as optional add-on modules rather than bundled into a tier, including the WhatsApp connector, the AI Assistant, Telephony Plus, and video calls, each priced separately per agent. A 14-day free trial is available on paid plans, letting teams test premium features like automation and multichannel routing before committing to a subscription.

features.title: Unified multichannel inbox
features.description: Website chat, WhatsApp, Telegram, Instagram, Facebook Messenger, Viber, email and calls in one shared agent inbox.

features.title: Proactive chat and visitor monitoring
features.description: See what page a visitor is browsing in real time and trigger proactive chat invitations to start conversations.

features.title: Built-in CRM
features.description: Lightweight CRM keeps lead details and full conversation history attached to each contact automatically.

features.title: AI Agent and chatbots
features.description: Optional AI Agent add-on and chatbot builder auto-answer common questions and qualify leads outside business hours.

features.title: Callback and offline forms
features.description: Visitors can request a callback or leave an offline message when no agent is available.

features.title: Telephony and video calls
features.description: Telephony Plus enables click-to-call, and a separate video call module supports face-to-face support sessions.

features.title: Chat API and mobile SDK
features.description: Enterprise plan exposes a Chat API and mobile SDK for embedding JivoChat inside custom websites and native apps.

features.title: Cross-platform apps
features.description: Native desktop apps for Windows and macOS plus iOS and Android apps keep agents synced wherever they work.

pros: Generous free plan supports up to 5 agents with no time limit
True multichannel inbox covering WhatsApp, Telegram, Instagram and more
Fast, straightforward widget installation on any website
Built-in CRM and canned responses reduce the need for extra tools
Native mobile and desktop apps keep agents responsive on the go
cons: WhatsApp connects via a paid third-party (Twilio-based) module rather than a native integration
Notification volume can feel excessive without tuning settings
Advanced features like video calls and the Chat API are locked behind the Enterprise tier
Per-agent pricing on annual billing adds up quickly for larger support teams
Support response times are reported as inconsistent on lower-tier plans

faqs.question: Is JivoChat free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. JivoChat offers a permanent Free plan for up to 5 agents with website chat, callback, offline forms and core social integrations. Paid plans starting around $28/agent/month add automation, CRM and multichannel messaging.

faqs.question: Does JivoChat support WhatsApp?
faqs.answer: Yes, WhatsApp is available as an integration on the Professional plan and above, delivered through a paid connector (Twilio or similar) rather than a free native integration.

faqs.question: Who owns JivoChat?
faqs.answer: JivoChat was founded by Timur Valishev in 2012 and was acquired by Russian banking group Sberbank in June 2021.

faqs.question: Does JivoChat have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, JivoChat provides native iOS and Android apps in addition to desktop apps for Windows and macOS, all synced in real time.

faqs.question: What is the JivoChat AI Agent?
faqs.answer: The AI Agent is an optional paid add-on that automatically answers common customer questions using JivoChat's chatbot and AI tooling, reducing the load on human agents.

faqs.question: What are the best JivoChat alternatives?
faqs.answer: Common alternatives include Tidio, Tawk.to, LiveChat, Intercom and Zendesk Chat, which compete on similar live chat and multichannel features at varying price points.

use_cases.title: Small e-commerce support
use_cases.description: Online stores add the JivoChat widget to answer pre-sale questions in real time and recover abandoned carts with proactive invitations.

use_cases.title: Multichannel customer support teams
use_cases.description: Support teams consolidate WhatsApp, Instagram and website chat into a single agent inbox to avoid missing messages across channels.

use_cases.title: Lead capture for service businesses
use_cases.description: Local service businesses use callback widgets and offline forms to capture leads outside business hours and route them into the built-in CRM.

enrichment.short_description: Jobber is field service management software that helps home service businesses schedule, quote, invoice and get paid.
enrichment.seo_title: Jobber Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jobber review: field service scheduling, quoting, invoicing and payments for home service businesses. See 2026 pricing tiers, pros, cons and alternatives compared.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Jobber is field service management software built for home service businesses such as plumbers, electricians, landscapers, cleaners, HVAC technicians and other on-site trades, covering the full job lifecycle from quoting through scheduling, invoicing and payment collection. The company was founded in 2011 in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada by Sam Pillar and Forrest Zeisler, who started the business after Pillar ran his own painting company and experienced firsthand how much time small service businesses lost to paper-based scheduling and invoicing; Jobber remains headquartered in Edmonton and has grown into one of the largest software employers in Western Canada, with reported headcount exceeding 1,000 employees as of 2026. Jobber has raised more than 200 million dollars in venture funding across multiple rounds from investors including Summit Partners, Version One Ventures and other growth-stage backers, and vendor-reported estimates place its annual recurring revenue above 100 million dollars, serving well over 200,000 home service professionals across North America. Architecturally, Jobber is a cloud-based SaaS platform accessible through a web dashboard and native iOS/Android mobile apps used by field technicians, syncing schedules, client records, and job data in real time between the office and the field; it integrates with more than 100 third-party tools including QuickBooks Online, Xero, Mailchimp and various payment processors. Core functionality spans client and property management, drag-and-drop scheduling and dispatching, route optimization for crews, professional quote and invoice generation with online approval and online payments, a client hub portal where customers can request work and pay invoices, automated appointment reminders and follow-ups, job costing and time tracking, two-way SMS messaging with clients, custom workflow automations, and performance reporting dashboards. Pricing is tiered by feature depth and user count rather than a simple per-seat model: the Core plan (roughly 21 to 30 dollars/month depending on commitment) supports a single user with online booking, quoting, invoicing and a business website; Connect (roughly 70 to 120 dollars/month) adds automated client reminders, automatic payment collection, checklists and QuickBooks sync; Grow (roughly 105 to 180 dollars/month) adds customizable visual quotes, upselling, automatic time tracking, job costing and workflow automation for up to 10 users; and Plus (roughly 280 to 420 dollars/month) is built for larger multi-crew operations with 5 to 15 users, centralized job files, team mentions, an AI Receptionist feature, a Marketing Suite, and dedicated onboarding support. Additional users can be added to any plan for around 29 dollars/month each, and Jobber offers a 14-day free trial with full access to Grow-tier features, no credit card required. Jobber's target audience is small-to-midsize home service companies, typically ranging from solo operators to multi-crew businesses with dozens of technicians, who need an all-in-one operations tool rather than stitching together separate scheduling, invoicing and payment apps. Its main competitors are Housecall Pro, ServiceTitan, FieldEdge and Workiz; Jobber differentiates through a strong reputation for ease of use and customer support, a lower price point than enterprise-focused ServiceTitan, and deep specialization in home services rather than broader field service or asset management use cases.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Jobber was founded in Edmonton, Alberta in 2011 by Sam Pillar and Forrest Zeisler after Pillar's own experience running a painting business exposed how much time home service companies lost to paper quotes, spreadsheets and manual invoicing. The platform grew into an all-in-one operations tool for trades like plumbing, HVAC, landscaping, cleaning and electrical work, covering everything from the first client request to the final paid invoice.

Jobber is a cloud-based SaaS product with a web dashboard for office staff and native mobile apps for field technicians, keeping schedules, client records, and job status synced in real time between the office and the truck. The company has raised over 200 million dollars in funding and now employs more than 1,000 people, serving hundreds of thousands of home service professionals across North America.

The product is squarely aimed at small-to-midsize home service businesses that need scheduling, quoting, invoicing and payments in one system rather than a patchwork of separate tools, and it competes most directly with Housecall Pro and the more enterprise-oriented ServiceTitan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Jobber's core workflow starts with client and property records, professional quotes that clients can approve online, and drag-and-drop scheduling and dispatching that route jobs to the right technician. Invoicing is built in with online payment collection, and a client hub portal lets customers request service, approve quotes, and pay invoices without a phone call.

Operational features include route optimization for multi-stop days, job checklists, automatic time tracking and job costing to see true profitability per job, two-way SMS for client communication, automated reminders and follow-up sequences, and custom workflow automation rules. Higher tiers add a centralized job file view for larger teams, team mentions for internal collaboration, an AI Receptionist that can field incoming calls, and a Marketing Suite for campaigns.

Jobber connects to more than 100 external tools, most notably QuickBooks Online and Xero for accounting sync, along with Mailchimp, Zapier and various payment processors, letting service businesses keep their existing bookkeeping workflow intact.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Jobber offers four tiers priced by feature depth and included user count. Core (about $21-$30/month) is built for solo operators and covers online booking, quoting, invoicing and a business website for one user. Connect (about $70-$120/month) adds automated reminders, automatic payment collection and QuickBooks sync. Grow (about $105-$180/month) adds visual quotes, upselling, automatic time tracking, job costing and workflow automation for up to 10 users, and Plus (about $280-$420/month) targets larger operations with 5-15 users, the Marketing Suite, AI Receptionist and dedicated onboarding.

Pricing varies depending on billing commitment (monthly, one-year commitment, or annual prepaid), and additional users can be added to any plan for roughly $29/month each. Jobber offers a 14-day free trial with full access to Grow-tier functionality and no credit card required, letting prospective customers test scheduling, invoicing and automation before subscribing.

features.description: Tracks technician time and expenses per job to reveal true profitability.
